name: Chinese cabbage (pe-tsai / Napa, Brassica rapa ssp. pekinensis) (food medium)

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Combustion N x factor; 1.2 g/100 g

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Horticulturae 2023, 9(12):1264 doi:10.3390/horticulturae9121264 (35 varieties, ion chromatography, fresh wt)
components.evidence: Mean 14.52 g/kg FW (range 10.09-21.14 g/kg). One of the two dominant carbon sources.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Mean 13.40 g/kg FW (range 7.72-28.26 g/kg). Co-dominant carbon source. ADDED (was missing from FooDB profile).

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Present but minor and below the glucose/fructose pool; not individually quantified in the 35-variety study (only Glc+Fru reported). USDA total sugars = 1.41 g/100 g.

components.name: malic acid (L-malate)
components.evidence: Mean 0.84 g/kg FW (range 0.22-2.43 g/kg). DOMINANT organic acid; a key positive nutritional-quality index. ADDED (missing from FooDB).

components.name: citric acid (citrate)
components.evidence: Mean 0.23 g/kg FW (range 0.06-1.18 g/kg; CV 91%, highly variable). ADDED (missing from FooDB).

components.name: oxalic acid (oxalate)
components.source: Horticulturae 2023, 9(12):1264 doi:10.3390/horticulturae9121264; corroborated ~4 mg/100 g by oxalatecontent.com
components.evidence: Mean 0.10 g/kg FW (range 0.04-0.20 g/kg). Low-oxalate vegetable. ADDED (missing from FooDB).

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: D-serine in current FooDB list is a stereochemistry artifact; the biological form is L-serine

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidates the FooDB 'Iron', 'Iron (Fe3+)', 'Fe2+ mitochondria' duplicate speciation entries

components.name: molybdenum (molybdate)
components.evidence: Trace essential micronutrient; present but not quantified for pe-tsai in USDA

components.name: cobalt (Co2+)
components.evidence: Trace micronutrient (B12 cofactor context); present but unquantified

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: Sulfur source; Brassica also carries S in glucosinolates; unquantified

components.name: vitamin C (L-ascorbate)
components.source: USDA FDC 169979 (corrob. ~25 mg/100 g Horticulturae 2023; 47 mg/100 g China FCT 2015)

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: 0.19 mg/100 g; provitamin A (16 ug RAE / 318 IU vitamin A)

components.name: retinol
components.evidence: Plant food: preformed retinol = 0; vitamin A activity is from beta-carotene

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: Plant food: 0 (FooDB list had no B12; confirmed absent)

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: 55 mg/100 g; dominant fatty acid in this low-fat leaf

components.name: nitrogen (total, as nitrate/organic N)
components.evidence: Leafy Brassica accumulates nitrate; present, unquantified

components.name: water / H2O
components.evidence: duplicate of proximate water; retained as the medium solvent

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: stereochemistry artifact; the biological amino acid in cabbage protein is L-serine (replaced by L-serine)

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: racemic/stereo artifact duplicate of L-isoleucine; FooDB predicted noise, not a distinct nutrient

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial (Streptomyces) fermentation product / food preservative; not a natural constituent of raw Chinese cabbage

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/Rutaceae; biologically implausible in a Brassica leaf matrix; FooDB predicted-not-measured

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: BiGG compartment-tagged duplicate of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: redox-speciation duplicate of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: ambient/metabolic gas, not a food-composition nutrient; supplied by the environment/exchange, not the food

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: ambient gas / electron acceptor, not a food-derived nutrient of the matrix

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: proton activity (pH) is a medium property, not a compositional nutrient of the food

missing_added: D-fructose (1.34 g/100 g) - was absent despite being a co-dominant sugar
malic acid (84 mg/100 g) - dominant organic acid, was entirely missing
citric acid (23 mg/100 g) - was missing
oxalic acid (10 mg/100 g) - was missing
L-phenylalanine, L-valine - essential AAs absent from the current list
quantitative amounts for glucose and all previously name-only components
keep_uncovered: pelargonidin (genuine anthocyanidins in colored/purple pe-tsai types)
glucosinolates (gluconapin, glucobrassicanapin, glucobrassicin - the defining Brassica S-metabolites; genuine, no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169979 - Cabbage, chinese (pe-tsai), raw (SR Legacy full nutrient panel: proximates, amino acids, minerals, vitamins, fatty acids)

Wang et al., Horticulturae 2023, 9(12):1264, doi:10.3390/horticulturae9121264 - Comprehensive Evaluation of Nutritional Qualities of Chinese Cabbage (35 varieties; ion-chromatography glucose, fructose, malic/citric/oxalic acid, minerals, fresh weight)
oxalatecontent.com - Napa cabbage oxalate (~4 mg/100 g), corroborating low-oxalate status
FooDB FOOD00035 - Chinese cabbage (trace minerals, betaine, phytochemical inventory)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are the free hexoses D-glucose (~1.45 g/100 g) and D-fructose (~1.34 g/100 g), together ~2.8 g/100 g on a fresh-weight basis (USDA's 'total sugars' 1.41 g/100 g reflects a different varietal/tissue average); the principal organic acid is malic acid (~84 mg/100 g) with lesser citrate and low oxalate. Nitrogen is available as low free/total amino acids (total free AA ~0.31 g/100 g per Horticulturae 2023), protein-bound AAs, plus nitrate/ammonium. The food is very low fat, so lipids are minor. Removed FooDB noise = stereo/racemic artifacts (D-serine, erythro-isoleucine), a microbial preservative (epsilon-polylysine), an implausible Apiaceae furanocoumarin (oxypeucedanin), BiGG iron-speciation duplicates, and ambient gases/proton (CO2, O2, H+); no heavy-metal contaminants were present in the source list.
